stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 30% and 47%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $165K in good weather patterns, $49K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $70K in good weather patterns, $15K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $207K in good weather patterns, $54K in bad weather patterns. corn: $207K in good weather patterns, $14K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.39: best option is soybeans with EV=$113.7K. soybeans is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [99.9, 125.9].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
soybeans is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
